page: Amazon Web Services i4g.xlarge is a storage-optimized instance powered by custom AWS Graviton processors. Built on the arm64 architecture, it features 4 dedicated physical cores utilizing the ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 model running at 2.5 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 32.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB per core, and a bundled 937 GB local NVMe SSD for high-speed storage. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 2 Gbps under the Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark data reveals a mixed performance profile. The instance achieves average results in stress-ng single-core (1520.08) and multi-core (6056.47) CPU tests, alongside average performance in Passmark database operations (2579.99) and large-block memory bandwidth tests (71.67 GB/sec read, 34.86 GB/sec write). However, it exhibits weak performance in single-core Geekbench (969.0) and multi-core Geekbench (3224.0) tests, as well as weak Redis SET operations (4.77 million operations/sec) and static web serving throughput (4.82 GB/sec). Cryptographic and LLM inference tasks show poor performance, with Passmark CPU encryption scoring 541.67 and small model text generation reaching 26.64 tokens/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the i4g.xlarge offers a balanced resource density for workloads requiring local NVMe storage and moderate memory capacity without the premium of x86 licensing. Given its average database operation benchmarks and high local storage capacity, this instance is suited for localized database workloads, caching layers, and storage-intensive applications that do not require intensive cryptographic or high-performance compute capabilities.
description: Amazon Web Services i4g.xlarge is a storage-optimized, arm64-based instance powered by 4 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 cores running at 2.5 GHz. It features 32.0 GB of RAM and a bundled 937 GB local NVMe SSD, operating on the Nitro hypervisor with a 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks indicate average capabilities in database operations and multi-core stress tests, though it exhibits weaker results in cryptographic, LLM inference, and single-core computational tasks. The qualitative cost efficiency of this instance is driven by its high local storage density and ARM-based architecture, making it a suitable option for storage-intensive applications, localized databases, and caching workloads that benefit from dedicated physical cores and local NVMe storage without requiring high-compute or specialized instruction sets.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i4g.xlarge is a storage-optimized arm64 instance with 4 Graviton cores, 32 GB RAM, and a 937 GB local NVMe SSD, designed for database and storage-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i4g.xlarge is an arm64 storage-optimized instance featuring 4 Graviton cores, 32 GB RAM, and a 937 GB local NVMe SSD.
tagline: An ARM-powered storage-optimized instance featuring dedicated physical cores, balanced memory density, and high-capacity local NVMe SSD storage.
bullet_points: Powered by 4 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 arm64 physical cores at 2.5 GHz
Equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory and a bundled 937 GB local NVMe SSD
Delivers average performance in database operations and large-block memory bandwidth
Exhibits lower performance in cryptographic, LLM inference, and single-core tasks
Best suited for localized databases, caching layers, and storage-intensive workloads
